title: X2-corpus FAIL 51/54: decoder drops optional SP slots / misorders referent intro under deep quote+op nesting (afraid, angry, sad)
notes:
Found by kernel-v0 corpus re-run (kernel-of-truth-138; poc/results/x2-kernel-v0-report.md). afraid: hard fail ERR_REF_NOT_INTRODUCED (ref 2 introduced out of order inside quote). angry: decoded AST drops "quant":"SOME" on an SP inside a quote (ok-but-wrong, minConfidence 0, step 'atomic-forced'). sad: drops an entire undergoer SP inside quote>NOT>CAN>DO nesting. IDENTICAL failures with minimal (referenced-ids-only) and full-corpus cleanup lexicons => NOT lexicon interference; it is presence-detection SNR under deep quote nesting. All 18 reference-bearing concepts decode exactly; failures are the quote-re-anchored emotion trio. Synthetic X2 gate (720/720 full grid) never generates these shapes — extend the generator or add authored-corpus regression fixtures.
Root-caused (decode-spec-fix agent): SPEC RULING — the afraid RECORD IS LEGAL and the VALIDATOR IS RIGHT per gist §4.2 ('exactly one introducing occurrence'; 'a cdef:ref to an undeclared or not-yet-introduced index is a gate error'; 'Quote-local referents (bound inside a cdef:quoteClauses) scope to that quote'): ref 2 is declared, bound exactly once in quote clause 0, and every mention follows it inside the same quote. The decoder never disagreed about the RULE — it DROPPED the introducing SP (decoded HAPPEN.roles={}), then failed its own output's validation. Same mechanism as angry/sad: at quote>op depth the true optional SP's presence signature (0.0335) sits BELOW pure-noise slots at the same depth (0.036-0.040) vs the 5-sigma gate 0.0552 — locally unseparable. FIX: decoder residual-completion phase (encoder/src/decoder.ts): after convergence, re-encode the decoded AST, subtract from v; missed content is then nearly the only residual content (measured 6-30x SNR at the missed sites: head 0.35-0.67, quant 0.33-0.68, bind ref:2 0.31, temporal op 0.46 vs noise ~0.02); probe every empty optional site there and accept a candidate only if the re-encoded edited clause explains the restored residual strictly better (exact matched-filter competition; phantoms cannot win). Encoder content-hash UNCHANGED (40e8c8ba...e2d10c); corpus UNCHANGED (no re-authoring, corpus hash stays f23f5211...); unit suite 38/38 (5 new regression tests incl. both validator ruling directions, encoder/test/corpus-regression.test.ts).
close_reason: X2-corpus re-run PASS: minimal 54/54 AND full-corpus 54/54 exact (was 51/54 both; report x2-kernel-v0-report.md 2026-07-07T04:22Z). Ruling: afraid record LEGAL, validator right (gist §4.2); decoder fixed via residual-completion phase. Encoder content-hash unchanged (40e8c8ba...e2d10c), corpus untouched (hash f23f5211... unchanged, no X1-corpus re-run needed), unit suite 38/38. Follow-ups filed: synthetic-gate re-run/generator coverage (P2), substitution/removal completion (P3).

Ambiguity-shadowed concepts: broken, lost, inside, near, kind can NEVER map under the abstain policy (every occurrence collides with a prime or another concept's inflection); needs a pre-registered policy decision — sense-priority tiers, concept renames, or measured WSD-assist-as-annotation

X2-q finding: decode at native D is 1/54 (512) / 8/54 (576) — expected per capacity math and ungated; IF a toy-native decode consumer ever appears, candidate mitigations to evaluate: thetaAbs tuning at coherent-crosstalk scale, resonator-style cleanup, depth<=2 restricted corpus

notes: poc/results/x2-report.md (720/720 PASS, 2026-07-07T03:16) was produced by the decoder at commit 0d21de1; the 0kn fix adds a residual-completion phase AFTER that evidence. Unit suite (38/38, incl. the sq2 depth-4x8 regression and the noise-vector phantom guard) plus X2-corpus cover it, but the synthetic 720-cell grid should be re-run for fresh gate evidence (node poc/dist/x2.js directly — do NOT npm run x2 while any poc/dist run is live; remove stale x2-checkpoint.json first or it resumes-as-done). Separately, the generator never emits the failing corpus shape (optional undergoer SP / SP-quant under quote>NOT/CAN/MAYBE>pred nesting) — extend it so the grid tests that region natively (sibling of kernel-of-truth-082's mod-diversity gap).

title: E2 extension (backlog, poc-design common rule 6): repeat E2 on one non-English model family — inputs/contexts are English-only; needs a translated context-bank design decision first.

E2 GPU launch (coordinator): run poc/gpu/setup-deploy-key.sh, register write deploy key (gh repo deploy-key add --allow-write), then poc/gpu/launch-e2.sh (spot, ~$1, self-terminating, pushes results/e2-<stamp> branch); revoke key after campaign. Dry-runs validated 2026-07-07.

E2: polarity-stratified subset reporting (X3 conditioning) — pre-registration conditions E2 interpretation on X3 polarity strata; runner defers it (poc/e2/README.md deviation 11). Add stratified partial-RSA reporting once X3 variant choice is settled.
X3-corpus: 13/79 meaning-inverting edits closer than nearest distinct pair; deep-quote attenuation; no notBoost variant dominates
notes: kernel-v0 corpus numbers (poc/results/x3-kernel-v0-report.md, kernel-of-truth-138): polarity flips median cos 0.9974 (max 0.99992, 'afraid' — flip buried in a quote), NOT-wrap median 0.8752, while meaning-PRESERVING alpha-renames median 0.7036 — the ordering a meaning-faithful similarity needs is inverted. 13 of 79 inverting edits leave a concept closer to its own negation than afraid<->sad (cos 0.9933), the nearest distinct authored pair; offenders are quote-heavy concepts (afraid, liar, promise, wrong, trustworthy, right...) — same deep-quote attenuation mechanism as the X2-corpus decode failures (kernel-of-truth-0kn). notBoost=2/4 widens NOT-wrap only (0.75/0.53) but cannot touch pred-level polarity flips and degrades geometry (RDM Spearman 0.989/0.939) => still no dominating variant; similarity-consumer ban stands (architecture.md 1.2, panel O9). Candidate work: polarity-aware treatment at the predicate level (WANT/DON'T-WANT, GOOD/BAD codes), and depth/quote weight compensation.

title: kernel-v0 coverage follow-up: primes TOUCH/ABOVE/BELOW/SIDE/HERE/A-SHORT-TIME/MOMENT and operator-form MORE/LIKE-over-clauses unused or thin; add concepts or widen existing ones if X-run lexicon coverage matters

profile-1 expressiveness gaps hit while authoring kernel-v0 (feed to gist rev-3 / profile-2 discussion): no benefactive ('happen TO x'), no clause anaphora ('because of THIS'), no habituality (approximated with MUCH~MANY), spatial primes lack relational valency frames (INSIDE/NEAR/FAR ride the manner adjunct), anchor-only AFTER/BEFORE not available as restrictedBy clause (worked around as AFTER(NOW, THERE-IS(t)))

kernel-v0: mint content-addressed ids (@jeswr/concept-hash) to replace placeholder urn:kernel-v0:* ids; needs the axioms layer + alias table (gist §5-§6); manifest.encoderContentHash already pins the encoder version used
Decoder: deep-quote SNR — within-clause local refinement (restore-self/peel-siblings inside decodeClause) to push exact recovery past the depth-4 x 8-clause boundary case (X2 719/720 in reduced run)
close_reason: Fixed in 0d21de1 (decoder-only). Root cause: decode-order SNR loss, not encoder capacity — unpeeled adjunct atoms + unpeeled sibling quote clause turned into noise on deep structured-child estimates (deep-chain corr 0.60->0.15), pushing the true atomic filler under the 5-sigma gate, after which the required-slot assumePresent path fabricated a phantom SP. Fix: two-phase atomic-first role sweep in decodeClause, deferred KIND/PART of-resolution in decodeSP, restore-self/peel-siblings refinement in decodeClauseList, and required-slot matched-filter competition (raw unbind scale) in decodeRole. X2 full grid 720/720 exact, pre-registered gate PASS; depth 5-6 also 100% with higher confidence. Encoder content-hash UNCHANGED (40e8c8ba...e2d10c); X0 goldens byte-identical; unit suite 31/31 incl. new regression test.

title: Decoder: deep-quote SNR — within-clause local refinement (restore-self/peel-siblings inside decodeClause) to push exact recovery past the depth-4 x 8-clause boundary case (X2 719/720 in reduced run)

title: Synth generator: emit multi-mod SPs so X3's mods-reorder preserving-edit class is non-empty; also widen small-shape (depth<=2, 1-clause) diversity
Re-run Phase X (X0-X4) on kernel v0 data once authored (current runs are synthetics-only; noted in each report)
close_reason: Phase X re-run on the AUTHORED kernel-v0 corpus (54 concepts, encoder 40e8c8ba..., corpus f23f5211...; poc/results/*-kernel-v0-report.* + kernel-v0-phase-x-summary.md, commit cae9af0). X1: SUCCESS — min adversarial single-edit angle 0.002342 rad over 2475 saturation-enumerated neighbours = 11.0x fp16 floor (0.000213 rad; bar >5x); min authored pair afraid<->sad 0.1156 rad — the emotion trio, NOT give/take/gift (repair<->take is rank 4; finding kernel-of-truth-7yp). X2: FAIL — 51/54 exact decode in BOTH minimal and full-corpus cleanup-lexicon conditions; afraid (hard fail, quote-scope referent order), angry+sad (optional SP slots dropped under deep quote+op nesting); all 18 reference-bearing concepts exact (bug kernel-of-truth-0kn). X3: 13/79 meaning-inverting edits closer than the nearest distinct pair; polarity-flip median cos 0.9974 vs meaning-preserving alpha-rename 0.7036; notBoost=4 costs RDM rho 0.939 without touching pred flips — no dominating variant, similarity ban stands (kernel-of-truth-5lh). X4: SUCCESS — RDM Spearman 0.9718 (8192->512) / 0.9706 (8192->576), min R^d margins 0.002252/0.002199 rad = 10.1x/9.9x floor under the pinned jl/8192/{512,576} streams; afraid<->sad remains min pair in R^d. Synthetic x1:full (parallel agent's run) untouched throughout.

title: Encoder: toy-native construction variant for D < 8192 (E1/E4 dimension policy path (i)); exact Hadamard level needs 13 index bits, so d_model-native re-encode needs a quasi-orthogonal codebook design + its own Phase-X rerun
close_reason: Variant kot-enc-Bq/1 shipped; both close gates passed: X0-q byte-determinism PASS (goldens committed, poc/fixtures/golden-vectors-q.json) and corpus adversarial margin bar >5x fp16 floor at native D passed at BOTH dims (11.3x @512, 9.6x @576; poc/results/x1-q-report.md). Codebook: deterministic Rademacher (slot,filler) atoms, coherence certified (qcert-report: full-space max |cos| 0.246@512/0.215@576 = random concentration target; corpus-realised 0.168/0.170). Content-hashes for coordinator to pin into poc-design.md: Bq/1@512 3492799ed73b49a612bebca920421041edd31d7bd4098bcf55da52df127ab9ee, Bq/1@576 6ad0b2bc01c06447aae8468f500e2a2178e9a40f0d95de048b0418afcaea77db. Decode at native D: 1/54 (512) and 8/54 (576) exact vs 54/54 at 8192 - expected capacity-math finding, ungated, reported (x2-q-report). RDM: native re-encode preserves full-D geometry as well as the JL projection (0.9711/0.9795 vs 0.9718/0.9706); native-vs-JL agreement 0.938/0.948 (x4-q-report).

Decoder residual completion is insertion-only: extend the global matched-filter competition to SUBSTITUTION/REMOVAL of wrong optional content
notes: The kernel-of-truth-0kn fix recovers MISSED optional content from the global residual (v minus re-encoded decode) via insertion candidates accepted only when the re-encoded edit explains the restored residual strictly better. ok-but-wrong decodes where content was decoded WRONG (wrong atom/quant/phantom optional slot) leave a +true/-decoded signature in the same residual and are amenable to the same acceptance test with substitution/removal candidate generation (probe filled optional sites for a strictly-better replacement, incl. dropping the filler). No known current failure needs it (X2-corpus 54/54 target after 0kn) — this is robustness for future corpora.

title: kernel-v0: nearest pair is the emotion trio (afraid<->sad 0.116 rad), not the deliberately-near give/take/gift cluster
notes: X1-corpus (poc/results/x1-kernel-v0-report.md, kernel-of-truth-138): all-pairs minimum is afraid<->sad at 0.1156 rad (cos 0.9933), then happy<->sad 0.2051, afraid<->happy 0.2238; the authored-to-be-near possession-transfer cluster first appears at rank 4 (repair<->take 0.4884) and give/take/gift themselves are not in the top 10 at all (top10 in the report json). The emotion trio shares nearly all clause structure (feel + quote re-anchoring), differing only in small deep-quote fragments that the encoder attenuates. Implication for corpus authoring: structural overlap dominates geometric proximity, not intended semantic nearness — worth a corpus README note and maybe a v0.2 authoring pass if margin work wants give/take to be the stress pair.
